response:
The inequality for the button area to be at least 10 square inches is 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 >= 10 which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 17 >= 0. Evaluating the function P(x) at the interval boundaries shows P(3) equals 16 and P(6) equals 251 while the minimum value within the interval [3, 6] occurs near x=3.5 where P(3.5) is approximately 23.5. Since the minimum value of 16 is greater than 10 the condition is satisfied for the entire range of x-values from 3 to 6 inches inclusive.

******

To find the screen width where the button area is 15 square inches we solve 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15 which simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0. Testing integer values reveals no integer roots but evaluating the function shows P(3) equals 16 and P(2) equals -11 indicating a real root exists between 2 and 3. Using numerical approximation methods the solution is approximately x = 2.94 inches.
